channel.title: RSS Aggregator Feed
channel.description: An aggregated feed of Technology-related news

item.title: Secretlab Magnus Evo standing desk review: Streamlined and refined but unmistakably Secretlab in spirit
item.description: The Secretlab Magnus Evo is a refined and simplified standing desk with a few nice features and none of the excess.

item.title: « Des prix excessifs » : Ticketmaster et Live Nation reconnus coupables de surfacturation
item.description: Live Nation et Ticketmaster ont été reconnus coupables de monopole et de surfacturation des billets par un jury fédéral à New York.

item.title: Anthropic releases Claude Opus 4.7, narrowly retaking lead for most powerful generally available LLM
item.description: Anthropic is publicly releasing its most powerful large language model yet, Claude Opus 4.7, today — as it continues to keep an even more powerful successor, Mythos, restricted to a small number of external enterprise partners for cybersecurity testing and patching vulnerabilities in the software said enterprises use (which Mythos exposed rapidly).The big headlines are that Opus 4.7 exceeds its most direct rivals — OpenAI's GPT-5.4, released in early March 2026, scarcely more than a month ago; and Google's latest flagship model Gemini 3.1 Pro from February — on key benchmarks including agentic...

item.title: Two Americans sentenced for helping North Korea steal $5 million in fake IT worker scheme
item.description: The U.S. Department of Justice announced that two Americans were sentenced to years in prison for helping the North Korean government place fake IT workers in U.S. companies.

item.title: Webb Telescope Resolves Cosmic Identity Crisis Between Planets and Stars
item.description: Where do massive planets end and stars begin? It may come down to how they formed.

item.title: Microsoft planning Surface Laptop with an OLED display
item.description: Microsoft is preparing to launch new Surface Pro and Surface Laptop models in the coming months. Windows Central reports that the refreshed models will include both Intel and Qualcomm variants, with Microsoft opting for an OLED display option for the Surface Laptop this year. Microsoft is expected to launch new Surface Pro and Laptop models […]

item.title: InsightFinder raises $15M to help companies figure out where AI agents go wrong
item.description: According to CEO Helen Gu, the biggest problem facing the industry today is not just monitoring and diagnosing where AI models go wrong, it's diagnosing how the entire tech stack operates now that AI is a part of it.

item.title: AI traffic to US retailers rose 393% in Q1, and it’s boosting their revenue too
item.description: Adobe says AI traffic to U.S. retail sites also jumped 269% in March, with visitors converting better and generating more revenue than non-AI shoppers.

item.title: Oubliez les groupes électrogènes : cette incroyable batterie mobile fonctionne sans une goutte d’essence
item.description: En Australie, le pétrole se raréfie, mais le solaire prend le relais. Face aux turbulences qui secouent les marchés du carburant, un fabricant australien pousse certaines entreprises à passer au vert grâce à son système de stockage solaire mobile.

item.title: Amazon's latest sale on its best-selling tech gadgets feels like Prime Day — up to 50% off TVs, Ring Doorbells, Fire tablets, and Blink cameras
item.description: Amazon is slashing prices on its best-selling tech gadgets, including Fire tablets, Ring Doorbells, Blink cameras, Fire Sticks, and more, starting at just $14.99.

item.title: 7 of the best Mac apps to level up your new MacBook Neo
item.description: Improve your MacBook Neo with these 7 top-rated macOS apps

item.title: 'Is AI impacting jobs right now? We’ve looked and, honestly, we haven’t seen it': LinkedIn exec says AI isn't causing a big drop-off in hiring just yet
item.description: AI may be causing a small drop in hiring - but LinkedIn says it's not too bad right now.

item.title: Toyota CUE7 : le robot basketteur qui apprend à dribbler et shooter… tout seul
item.description: Annoncé le 12 avril 2026 à Tokyo, CUE7 est bien plus qu'un robot capable de marquer des paniers. Derrière la démonstration sportive se cache une rupture technologique majeure dans les travaux de Toyota sur l'intelligence artificielle et la robotique humanoïde.

item.title: The ‘Avatar’ Movie Leak Didn’t Come From a Paramount Email, But It’s Still Just as Bad
item.description: A new report puts a spotlight on the aftermath of one of the biggest leaks Paramount has faced in years.

item.title: Google Makes Image Generation a Little Creepier With Personal Intelligence
item.description: Solving a problem no one had.

item.title: Microsoft’s new Xbox chief starts making her mark
item.description: Microsoft's new Xbox chief has had a busy couple of months after promising "the return of Xbox." Asha Sharma met with publishers at the Game Developers Conference in March, and has also been on the road visiting Microsoft's own game studios and product teams in recent weeks. Sharma, who used to work in Microsoft's CoreAI […]

item.title: Gemini can now pull from Google Photos to generate personalized images
item.description: Google's Personal Intelligence feature, which lets Gemini pull data from apps like Google Photos to offer responses tailored to you, can now use that data and its Nano Banana 2 image model to create images based on your personal context. With the feature, you can use prompts like "Design my dream house" or "Create a […]

item.title: Roblox’s AI assistant gets new agentic tools to plan, build, and test games
item.description: The new new tools are designed to help creators throughout the entire development process.

item.title: Google adds Nano Banana-powered image generation to Gemini’s Personal Intelligence
item.description: The feature will be initially available to paid users in the U.S.

item.title: Gemini can now draw on your Google data to personalize the images it generates
item.description: Your Google Photos library could soon influence the kind of images you can generate with Gemini. After letting users personalize the AI assistant's responses with data from Gmail, Search and YouTube, Google says it's bringing that same "Personal Intelligence" to Nano Banana 2 to make it easier for users to create personalized images with the AI model.The goal is to have the data affiliated with your Google account — your YouTube history, emails, Google Photos, etc. — provide context to Nano Banana 2 so you don't have to. Rather than prompting Gemini's image generation model with information ab...

item.title: Anthropic releases a new Opus model amid Mythos Preview buzz
item.description: Anthropic has released its most powerful "generally available" model to date: Claude Opus 4.7. The company called it a step up from Opus 4.6 for advanced software engineering tasks, particularly in complex coding areas that in the past required more hand-holding. It's also supposed to be better at analyzing images and following instructions, and it […]...

item.title: Rivals season 2 trailer looks bonk-bustingly brilliant — but we've got to wait months for the full Hulu and Disney+ series to release as series splits in two
item.description: I'm incredibly excited for Rivals season 2 after its chaotic trailer reveal... but why is the Hulu and Disney+ series being split into two batches?

item.title: Could This AI-Simulated Brain Lead to Human Mind-Uploading?
item.description: We’re not holding our breath.

item.title: James Bond 007 First Light PS5 controller pre-order build-up live — the best early links to bookmark and info you need
item.description: We've got all the best information and early links to bookmark ahead of the James Bond 007 First Light limited edition DualSense pre-orders this week.

item.title: 'Anyone with $10 could have walked straight through': Report warns this legit-looking software is actually antivirus-killing adware
item.description: Annoying adware turned out to be a piece of malware capable of killing antivirus software.

item.title: Face à Qualcomm, Intel envoie son 18A jusque dans les PC à bas prix
item.description: C'est une première : avec ses Core Series 3 lancés ce jeudi, Intel fait descendre son process le plus avancé dans les laptops d'entrée de gamme. Une rupture que la pression infligée par Qualcomm et la menace AluminiumOS rendent inévitable.

item.title: The first real trailer for the Street Fighter movie is filled with crowd-pleasing moments
item.description: We finally have a real-deal trailer for the upcoming Street Fighter movie, after a short teaser dropped at The Game Awards last year. This is nearly three minutes of fighting, silly dialogue and, of course, Easter eggs from the games. To the latter point, there's a scene of Ken beating up a car like in the bonus stages from Street Fighter II and footage of Ryu powering up one of his famous Hadoken fireballs. There's even a cheeky reference to Chun-Li's notoriously-large and powerful thighs. This is all helped along by the fact that the actors all look very silly and mostly accurate to the game...

item.title: How Netflix made us fall in love with K-dramas
item.description: This is Lowpass by Janko Roettgers, a newsletter on the ever-evolving intersection of tech and entertainment, syndicated just for The Verge subscribers once a week. What do you get if you take a bunch of ripped, shirtless male K-pop idols in boxing gloves and have them spar in the ring until they're sweating? For Netflix: […]

item.title: Meta isn't setting its Oversight Board free just yet
item.description: The Oversight Board — the policy body Meta created to weigh its most impactful moderation rulings — has seen its role within Mark Zuckerberg's empire come into question due to shifting content policy priorities and dwindling investment. The Oversight Board has taken steps to formalize its long-contemplated desire to work with other companies, but Engadget has learned Meta has thus far declined to move forward with that process. Over the last year, board members have become increasingly interested in artificial intelligence policy and how their experience shaping Meta's content rules could tran...

item.title: Meta Quest headset prices are going up on April 19
item.description: The RAM crisis has prompted another company to jack up hardware prices. Meta says it will be increasing the price of Quest headsets on April 19. The Meta Quest 3 will get a $100 hike to $599, while the Quest 3S will be $50 more expensive at $350 (for a version with 128GB of storage) and $450 (256GB). Meta is blaming the increases on the rising costs of RAM, which has skyrocketed in price due to a shortage of chips as AI companies gobble up as much memory as they can for their data centers. Sony recently bumped up the prices of PS5 consoles and the PlayStation Portal handheld for similar reason...

item.title: It’s slushy season, and Ninja’s frozen drink machine is nearly half off
item.description: Woot is making it more affordable to own a frozen drink machine. Ninja’s Slushi that has an 88-ounce container for storing your ice-cold creations is down to $184.99 at Woot, which is a whopping 47 percent off its list price. The Slushi requires no ice, just the liquid of your choosing and a little time […]

item.title: Ex-PlayStation boss says Microsoft is 'trying so hard to will' Xbox Game Pass 'into health' and suggests 'a clarifying post mortem would do the entire industry some good'
item.description: Former PlayStation executive Shawn Layden has suggested Microsoft can't save Xbox Game Pass, following comments made by Xbox CEO Asha Sharma about wanting to "evolve" the service.

item.title: Roku hits a major milestone with 100 million users
item.description: Roku continues to solidify itself in a very busy streaming landscape. As of April, over 100 million households are streaming with Roku devices, including its streaming sticks and boxes and Roku TVs. Roku originally spun out of Netflix in 2008, where it was conceived as an in-house streaming device. It's not just Roku that has […]

item.title: Warren Buffett once said that success at the end of your life comes down to 1 word
item.description: Don’t wait till the end of your life because it will be too late. The Buffett formula for success starts now. Warren Buffett is seldom wrong, especially regarding investment and innovation. As most of us know, the Oracle of Omaha offers wisdom that goes beyond industries, generations, and cultures.

item.title: Anna's Archive told to pay Spotify and record labels $322 million over unprecedented music scraping
item.description: The open-source library and search engine Anna’s Archive has been ordered to pay Spotify and the three of the world’s largest music labels $322 million in damages after it claimed to have scraped the entirety of the streaming platform’s library of music. Spotify, Universal Music Group, Warner Music Group and Sony Music Entertainment, sued Anna’s Archive in January for a slightly comical $13 trillion. They alleged Anna's Archive had illegally scraped 86 million songs — a significant chunk of all the music on the planet — and intended to make them available for download via BitTorrent. At the ti...

item.title: Meta’s Quest Price Hikes Just Put VR in a Worse Spot
item.description: The Quest 3S and 3 are getting more expensive, and memory prices are to blame.

item.title: ROG Xbox Ally X : la console d’Asus profite d’une grosse baisse de prix et devient bien plus accessible
item.description: La console portable d’Asus en partenariat avec Microsoft, la ROG Xbox Ally X, profite d’une belle remise et tombe à 441 euros contre 599 euros de base. Une belle opportunité pour la machine de jeu portable sous Windows.

item.title: Grâce à l’intelligence artificielle | Google affirme neutraliser des milliards de « publicités malveillantes »
item.description: Le recours à son modèle d’intelligence artificielle (IA) générative Gemini a permis à Google de bloquer en 2025 environ 8,2 milliards de publicités en ligne ayant enfreint ses règles, selon des chiffres publiés jeudi par le géant de la tech américaine.

item.title: Bluesky is having some issues in one of its 'reginos'
item.description: Bluesky is once again having a wobble. The platform says some of its systems are down and that it’s “investigating an incident with service in one of our reginos” (that’s Bluesky’s typo, not mine). The issue appears to have started at 1:42AM ET and was still persisting as of 11AM.According to Bluesky’s status page, three servers were down, including one based in the US East region. The outages appear to be due to connection timeout issues. It seems that only certain users are being affected. Bluesky has been mostly working fine for me this morning, though there were a couple of occasions when ...

item.title: D-Link Aquila Pro AI R95: high-speed Wi-Fi 7 at a competitive price
item.description: Forget about the eye-catching design – the D-Link Aquila Pro AI R95 router gets all the basics right, with excellent performance, versatile connectivity, and admirable ease of use.

item.title: iOS 27 : Apple préparerait 4 grandes nouveautés pour Apple Intelligence
item.description: À quelques semaines de l'ouverture de la WWDC 2026, la machine à rumeurs de Cupertino s'emballe. Un développeur a découvert dans le code d'applications iOS quatre nouvelles fonctionnalités à venir qu’Apple pourrait déployer avec iOS 27.

item.title: The 5 Coolest Genre Moments at Universal’s CinemaCon Panel
item.description: From Robert Eggers' 'Werwulf,' to the Minions and 'Odyssey,' there was a lot of awesome stuff at Universal's panel.

item.title: Google is now targeting bad ads over bad actors
item.description: Google blocked 8.3 billion ads in 2025 but suspended fewer advertisers.

item.title: Battlefield 6 is getting its 'biggest map' in response to player feedback, developer says it's 'nearly four times the size of Mirak Valley'
item.description: Battlefield Studios has detailed the next season of Battlefield 6 and beyond.

item.title: Think the PS5 Pro is too expensive? This infamously pricey gaming console is coming back from the dead
item.description: SNK and Plaion are bringing back the Neo Geo AES console.

item.title: Miss Succession? Brian Cox's new movie Glenrothan will make you rethink dysfunctional family dynamics all over again — and is a world away from Waystar Royco
item.description: Nothing can top HBO Max hit Succession for me — but for his directorial debut, Brian Cox might have come close with new movie Glenrothan.

item.title: For her ‘Confessions’ sequel, Madonna takes Helvetica to the club
item.description: The creative agency behind ‘brat’ did the typography for Madonna’s forthcoming ‘Confessions on a Dance Floor 2.’ Madonna announced her new album Confessions on a Dance Floor II with sans-serif typography from the same creative agency behind Charli XCX’s brat.

item.title: Nearly two-thirds of parents support their Gen Z kids financially, survey finds
item.description: Rising costs and shaky job prospects are keeping young adults financially dependent. According to Wells Fargo’s recent Money Study, 64% of parents with Gen Z children say their 18- to 28-year-old kids still rely on them for financial support—whether it’s for housing or other expenses.

item.title: You’ve heard of hybrid cars. Now meet a hybrid cement plant.
item.description: Why choose fossil fuels or electricity when you can have both? NOC Energy has figured out how to hybridize heavy industry.

item.title: This New Image of Mars Is Not the Same Thing NASA Saw in 1976
item.description: A new image shows a striking contrast between Mars' desert-like terrain against dark deposits of volcanic ash.

item.title: Students can get Microsoft 365 Premium and Xbox Game Pass Ultimate for free — but is it better than a MacBook Neo?
item.description: Students can get one year of Microsoft 365 Premium and Xbox Game Pass Ultimate for free with the purchase of a Windows 11 laptop. But is that enough to sway you away from the MacBook Neo?

item.title: Quelle est la meilleure dashcam à choisir en 2026 ?
item.description: Les caméras embarquées de voiture peuvent être très utiles pour certains conducteurs. Voyons dans ce dossier quelles sont les références les plus intéressantes du marché… et quel sera le meilleur choix de dashcam pour votre voiture.

item.title: Les nouvelles Microsoft Surface approchent : ce qu’il faut savoir
item.description: Des fuites dévoilent les caractéristiques des prochains PC de Microsoft, la gamme Microsoft Surface de 2026.

item.title: Gatorade, the inventor of the sports drink, is making a surprising pivot to reach non-athletes
item.description: The move reflects U.S. consumers’ surging interest in wellness. Sixty years after it invented sports drinks, Gatorade is making a surprising pivot: It’s no longer focusing primarily on athletes.PepsiCo, Gatorade’s parent company, said Thursday that the brand wants to broaden its reach to non-athletes who are looking for ways to hydrate, whether they’re on a long flight, going for a walk or nursing a hangover. New packaging highlights the specific ways Gatorade’s various drinks and powders work and the research behind them.The change reflects U.S. consumers’ booming interest in beverages with ...

item.title: 'It's a lot of fun': Blackmagic just turned the Apple Watch into the ultimate remote for iPhone filming
item.description: Version 3.3 of the iOS app adds camera controls and monitoring from your wrist.

item.title: J’ai testé la recharge en 5 minutes de la Denza Z9GT de BYD en France : mission accomplie, mais avec quelques contraintes
item.description: La Denza Z9GT, nouvelle voiture électrique du groupe BYD, arrive en France avec une promesse forte : une recharge 5 minutes grâce à une borne spécifique. Nous avons été témoins de cette recharge express, avant de tester ses performances de charge sur une borne Ionity. Les résultats sont hors du commun.

item.title: Les Galaxy S24 auraient droit à trois fonctions exclusives des Galaxy S26 : voici lesquelles
item.description: Avec One UI 8.5, Samsung prévoirait de déployer des fonctions uniquement utilisables sur les Galaxy S26 aux Galaxy S24 d'il y a deux ans.

item.title: Zelenskyy Says Drones Are the Future of War After Claiming Robot Unit Captured Russian Troops
item.description: "The story of Apple and many of the world’s top companies began the same way," Zelenskyy said of Ukraine's bootstrapped war industry.

item.title: An ancient Microsoft Excel security flaw could let hackers hijack your entire system, so patch now
item.description: CISA is warning about ongoing exploitation of a 2008 bug in multiple versions of Excel.

item.title: You can now turn off YouTube shorts entirely
item.description: If you're tired of YouTube Shorts, or if watching them has become a brain rot problem, you can now eliminate them on mobile.

item.title: On croirait un dessin, c’est pourtant bien une vraie voiture : Renault réinvente sa Twingo électrique
item.description: Le constructeur automobile français continue de mélanger culture populaire et industrie. Renault vient de confier sa nouvelle citadine électrique à un célèbre artiste américain pour une transformation en direct sur les Champs-Élysées. Sous ses coups de pinceau, la petite voiture est passée du monde réel à l'univers fascinant du croquis en noir et blanc....

item.title: Age verification is a mess but we’re doing it anyway
item.description: In the span of a few years, age verification went from an idea to standard practice on large parts of the internet. Seeking to prevent kids from accessing porn, other inappropriate content, or social media altogether, laws mandating age-gating have spread rapidly across the globe, reaching the UK, the US, Australia, France, Brazil, and many […]

item.title: 6 mindset shifts to improve your risk and failure tolerance
item.description: Thriving in an uncertain work future isn’t about knowing the most, but about being willing to learn, fail, and reinvent yourself as the circumstances require. It seems that change and volatility are the only things that are certain when it comes to the labor market. Jobs and professions that once seemed ‘stable’ are not immune to the forces of artificial intelligence and other technological advancements. At the very least, AI is changing the nature of what jobs look like and will likely continue to do so at a fast rate. All of this can make it difficult to know what to do to foolproof your ca...
